scan.title: Structure generation (pyxtal, smact, mattergen, diffcsp) as used by AtomisticSkills
scan.kind: fresh survey of a code family (structure PRODUCERS), catalog not map-code
scan.target: the structure-generation family exercised by AtomisticSkills (arXiv 2605.24002): how generative / random / screening operators enter a map whose Structure node is today a pure SOURCE with no producing edge
scan.family_framing: Four codes with THREE distinct output types. STRUCTURE PRODUCERS: mattergen (diffusion, property/chem-system conditioned), diffcsp (DiffCSP++ diffusion, space-group+Wyckoff constrained CSP or unconditional), and the AIRSS-style random search (pymatgen, NOT pyxtal). COMPOSITION FILTER (dimension-free screening, NOT a structure producer): smact (charge-balance / electronegativity validity). SYMMETRY HELPER (not a producer of anything on the map): pyxtal, used only for its space-group / Wyckoff operator tables inside the diffcsp wrapper.

sources.packages_importable: NONE of the four import under /Users/juicy/miniconda3/bin/python (ModuleNotFoundError for pyxtal, smact, mattergen, diffcsp); mattergen and diffcsp are git-only (mattergen: `uv pip install -e .` from a cloned repo; DiffCSP++: cloned to /home/bdeng/projects/DiffCSP-PP). pyxtal and smact are pip installable but were NOT downloaded: every functional fact below is anchored to committed AtomisticSkills usage, which is sufficient because pyxtal/smact are env-only there.
sources.atomistic_skills: AtomisticSkills/.agents/skills/ (vendored) plus src/ wrappers and MCP servers
skills: ml-generative-adit (ADiT latent diffusion; sibling producer, same downstream)
mat-random-structure-search (AIRSS-style; uses pymatgen Structure.from_spacegroup, NOT pyxtal)
mat-ionic-substitution (pymatgen substitution probability; charge balance via pymatgen, NOT smact)
mat-structure-novelty (StructureMatcher-based novelty filter, the downstream gate)
env_docs: AtomisticSkills/conda-envs/mattergen-agent/README.md (MatterGen: uv pip install -e . from cloned repo; py3.10; CUDA13; HF-hub checkpoints)
AtomisticSkills/conda-envs/diffcsp-agent/README.md (DiffCSP++: pip deps incl pyxtal + smact; py3.11; checkpoints via gdown; repo at /home/bdeng/projects/DiffCSP-PP)

AtomisticSkills/.agents/rules/mcp-environments.md (diffcsp-agent Core: DiffCSP++, hydra, PyG, pyxtal; mattergen-agent Core: MatterGen, PyG, lightning; adit-agent Core: ADiT ... pyxtal; smol-agent Core: smol)

graph_snapshot.structure_node: Structure (kind: symbolic). Quantity tag `structure` in omai/operator/registry.py:86: 'Atomic structure: cell, species, and positions (opaque in Phase 1).'
graph_snapshot.structure_is_pure_source: CONFIRMED. No edge in the map produces Structure. graph.json carries 0 edges (edges live in the operator layer, not the doc data); grepping the Structure node across producers shows it only ever CONSUMED (relax, solve_ground_state, phonon, MD all take Structure in). Today Structure is a root SOURCE node. A generative producer would be the FIRST edge into Structure.
graph_snapshot.records_148_153_note: The config-thermo encode the brief warned might land (records 148-153) had NOT landed at scan time; node_count is 77 (was 74 at the phonopy-lammps review). Not racing that encode.

entries.name: mattergen-generate-unconditional
entries.quantity: Novel inorganic crystal STRUCTURES sampled from a diffusion model's learned distribution (cell + species + positions), written as CIF
entries.units: dimensionless artifact (a Structure); metadata carries model_name, batch_size, guidance_scale

skill_usages.what: self.generator.generate(batch_size, num_batches, output_dir) -> list of pymatgen Structures, each written struct.to(cif). Model loaded from HF hub via MatterGenCheckpointInfo.from_hf_hub (mattergen_wrapper.py:78).

MCP generate_structures(num_structures, model_name='mattergen_base', guidance_scale=0.0 unconditional). Public pretrained models: mattergen_base, mp_20_base, dft_mag_density, chemical_system (mattergen_wrapper.py:34-39).

maps_to.status: producer-of-existing-source (PROPOSAL: representation-only provenance, NOT a new node)
maps_to.notes: Output IS a Structure. The interesting question the brief poses. Recommendation: a generated structure is EVIDENCE of a Structure whose SOURCE is the generator; the generator identity (mattergen_base, guidance_scale, seed) is representation/provenance metadata on the Structure instance, not a new physics node. See generative_operator_proposal in summary.

entries.name: mattergen-generate-conditioned
entries.quantity: Structures conditioned on a target property or chemical system (guided diffusion). chemical_system controls which ELEMENTS appear (not stoichiometry); dft_mag_density conditions on a scalar property
entries.symbol: S_gen ~ p_theta(S | c), c in {chemical_system, dft_mag_density}
entries.units: dimensionless artifact; the conditioning target carries the property's own units (e.g. magnetic density)

skill_usages.what: chemical_system='Li-Zr-Cl' auto-selects the chemical_system model, sets properties={'chemical_system':...}, bumps guidance_scale to 1.0. properties={'dft_mag_density':0.15} conditions on the fine-tune target.

chemical_system controls ELEMENTS not exact stoichiometry (Li-Zr-Cl can yield Li3Cl5, LiZrCl4, ...); CSP mode (exact composition) needs non-public CSP-trained models. Post-filter CIFs by exact elements is MANDATORY.
fine-tune conditions on an arbitrary scalar property column (e.g. formation_energy) from prepare_training_data.py CSV; produces a property-conditioned checkpoint.

maps_to.notes: The conditioning target (a property value, a chemical system) is a REPRESENTATION note on the generated Structure's provenance, not an edge FROM the property node. Physically the diffusion is p(S|c); on the map c is a label on the source annotation, not a consumed field (the property is a training-time steering target, not an evaluated input). Guidance_scale is a discretization/steering scheme parameter.

entries.name: diffcsp-generate-csp-symmetry
entries.quantity: Predicted crystal STRUCTURES with exact composition control from space group + Wyckoff letters + atom types (DiffCSP++ symmetry-constrained CSP), written as CIF
entries.units: dimensionless artifact; inputs: spacegroup in [1,230], Wyckoff multiplicities, element per site; step_lr Langevin step size

skill_usages.what: generate_structures_with_symmetry(spacegroup, wyckoff_letters, atom_types, model_name='mp_csp', step_lr=1e-5) -> num_generated CIFs. CSP models (mp_csp, perov_csp, mpts_csp) require atom_types; Gen models do not.

_build_syminfo_data uses `from pyxtal.symmetry import Group`: Group(spacegroup)[letter].ops gives the affine matrices of each Wyckoff site; anchor_index / ops_inv feed the PyG Data the diffusion consumes. This is the ONLY functional pyxtal call in the whole skill set.

maps_to.notes: Output is a Structure. The space-group + Wyckoff spec is an INPUT constraint (a symmetry label on the generation), not a map node consumed as a field. See space_group_as_node open question: SG number is a categorical annotation on the generated Structure, likely representation-only, not a new quantity node.

entries.name: diffcsp-generate-unconditional
entries.quantity: Ab-initio (unconditional) crystal structures from the DiffCSP++ training distribution (mp_gen, perov_gen, carbon_gen), no composition specified

skill_usages.what: unconditional_generate.py --model mp_gen --num_structures 100 --step_lr 5e-6; Required packages listed as torch, pyxtal, pymatgen, hydra-core (pyxtal an env dep, not a call here).

maps_to.notes: Same as mattergen unconditional. A diffusion-sampled Structure whose source is the DiffCSP++ Gen checkpoint.

entries.name: adit-generate-crystals
entries.quantity: Crystals (CIF) OR molecules (XYZ) from a unified latent diffusion model (ADiT, ICML 2025); unconditional only in the public checkpoint
entries.symbol: S_gen ~ p_theta(S) over the shared MP20+QM9 latent

skill_usages.what: mcp_adit_generate_structures(generation_type='crystals', cfg_scale=2.0) -> crystal CIFs. Public checkpoint is UNCONDITIONAL only; to hit a composition, generate-many-and-filter. Included as the fourth diffusion producer sibling: same map treatment.

maps_to.status: producer-of-existing-source (representation-only provenance); NOTE also produces Molecule (XYZ), off today's crystal-only map
maps_to.notes: Not in the brief's four codes but the same generative-producer class; cataloged so the proposal covers the whole family. The molecule branch has no map node (Phase 1 is crystalline).

entries.name: random-structure-search-airss
entries.quantity: Random symmetric crystal structures per sampled space group with randomized lattice + random fractional Wyckoff coords, filtered by minimum interatomic distance (AIRSS-style)
entries.symbol: S_rand(SG, V) with atoms at P1 random fractional coords
entries.units: dimensionless artifact; CIF + generation_manifest.json (spacegroup, volume_scale, num_atoms, volume)

skill_usages.what: Uses pymatgen Composition/Lattice/Structure + pymatgen.symmetry.groups.SpaceGroup.from_int_number, NOT pyxtal. Random lattice params per crystal system, random FRACTIONAL coords (coords_are_cartesian=False), min-distance filter = 0.7*(sum covalent radii). SKILL.md:82 admits 'No symmetry enforcement: atoms at random positions (P1); symmetry emerges only after relaxation.'

maps_to.notes: TRAP CORRECTION for the brief: the AIRSS-style random search does NOT use pyxtal. pyxtal (from_random / symmetry-constrained random structures) is NOT called by any AtomisticSkills skill. This skill is the map-relevant 'random symmetric structure producer', and it is pymatgen. Its structures are FRACTIONAL-coord, P1, pre-relaxation inputs.

entries.name: smact-composition-screening
entries.quantity: Composition VALIDITY verdicts: charge-neutral + electronegativity-ordered element/oxidation-state combinations. A FILTER, not a structure producer. DIMENSION-FREE screening
entries.symbol: valid(composition) in {True, False}; a boolean per (elements, oxidation-states) tuple

skill_usages.what: smact is an INSTALLED DEPENDENCY of diffcsp-agent, adit-agent, and mattergen-agent, but NO skill SCRIPT calls smact.screening or smact anywhere. grep 'smact' across .agents/skills/ returns ZERO functional call sites. It rides along as a DiffCSP++/ADiT/MatterGen upstream requirement.

skill_usages.skill: mat-ionic-substitution (the nearest charge-balance behavior, but via pymatgen NOT smact)
skill_usages.what: The map-relevant charge-balance/oxidation-state screening that DOES run uses pymatgen's AutoOxiStateDecorationTransformation + substitution probability model, and returns only charge-balanced substitutions. This is the pymatgen analog of smact's job; smact itself is not invoked.

maps_to.notes: smact outputs a dimension-free boolean over compositions: it is a FILTER on the composition space, not a producer of any physical quantity or Structure. It has no field, no dimension, no index. It is NOT map-node material. If a validity/screening annotation is ever wanted, it is a categorical label on a Composition, not a physics node. Cataloged as NOT-DRIVEN in AtomisticSkills and dimension-free by nature.

entries.name: pyxtal-symmetry-helper
entries.quantity: Space-group / Wyckoff-position OPERATOR TABLES (affine matrices per Wyckoff site). A symmetry lookup, not a producer of anything on the map

skill_usages.what: from pyxtal.symmetry import Group; Group(spacegroup)[letter].ops supplies the Wyckoff affine matrices that build the PyG Data the DiffCSP++ diffusion consumes. Pure symmetry bookkeeping INSIDE the diffcsp producer; pyxtal produces no CIF and no structure itself.
'Use ICSD, Materials Project, or pyxtal to find [space group + Wyckoff positions]' - documentation pointer, not an automated call.

maps_to.status: not a producer / not map-node material
maps_to.notes: The brief framed pyxtal as 'symmetry-constrained random structures'. In AtomisticSkills pyxtal is NOT used to generate structures at all - its pyxtal.from_random capability is unused. It is used only as a Wyckoff-operator TABLE inside diffcsp's wrapper. It produces nothing on the map. Space-group / Wyckoff appears only as an INPUT CONSTRAINT to diffcsp, handled as a representation label.

mattergen.note: unconditional + conditioned (chemical_system / property). Both PRODUCE Structure; proposal = representation-only provenance, not a new node.

diffcsp.note: symmetry-constrained CSP (mp_csp/perov_csp/mpts_csp) + unconditional Gen (mp_gen/perov_gen/carbon_gen). Both PRODUCE Structure.

pyxtal.note: Wyckoff-operator table inside diffcsp only; pyxtal.from_random UNUSED; produces nothing on the map.

smact.note: env-only dependency, ZERO skill call sites; a dimension-free composition-validity FILTER, not a physics quantity, not a Structure producer.

family_extras.note: adit (crystals+molecules) and the AIRSS-style random search (pymatgen) are the other two map-relevant Structure producers; cataloged so the generative-producer proposal covers the whole family.

generative_operator_proposal.the_question: Structure is today a pure SOURCE node with NO producing edge (confirmed: no edge in the map produces Structure). A generate_structure edge producing Structure would INVERT that topology - it would make Structure the first target of a producing edge. Does the map want generative producers as EDGES into Structure, or as REPRESENTATION-ONLY provenance on Structure instances?
generative_operator_proposal.recommendation: REPRESENTATION-ONLY provenance, NOT a producing edge into Structure (at least in Phase 1).
facts_gathered: FACT 1 - generated structures are INPUT artifacts, never evaluated outputs. In EVERY skill and the halide workflow the generated CIF is immediately RELAXED by an MLIP (mat-random-structure-search step 2 mcp_mace_relax_structure; halide workflow step 3 FairChem UMA relax), then E_hull-screened (mat-stability), then DFT-refined, then MD'd. The generator's raw output is never itself a physics result; it is the seed of a downstream evaluation chain. generative-halide-discovery.md:20-25.

FACT 2 - the physics content of a generated structure is UNVERIFIED at birth. SKILL docs repeatedly warn generated structures have 'varying levels of stability' and MUST be validated by relaxation + stability (mattergen SKILL.md:154; adit SKILL.md:103; random-search SKILL.md:81-85). A map node carries a defined quantity with identity; a raw generated structure carries only a proposal.

FACT 3 - Structure is OPAQUE in Phase 1 (registry.py:86: 'cell, species, and positions (opaque in Phase 1)'). The map does not yet decompose Structure into fields, so a 'generate_structure' operator would produce an opaque token - it would encode provenance, not physics.

FACT 4 - the conditioning is a training/steering target, not a consumed field. mattergen p(S|c): c (chemical_system, dft_mag_density, guidance_scale) steers the diffusion; it is NOT an evaluated input field the way Structure feeds solve_ground_state. Modeling it as an edge FROM a property node would falsely claim the property is computed-then-consumed.

FACT 5 - the precedent: solve_ground_state / relax CONSUME Structure and PRODUCE quantities (TotalEnergy, Forces, relaxed Structure). Those are physics operators whose output has definite identity. A generator has no physics input to consume (unconditional) or only a steering label (conditioned); it is categorically different from relax.
generative_operator_proposal.the_provenance_model: Treat a generated structure as EVIDENCE of a Structure (a Structure instance) whose SOURCE is the generator. The generator identity - {code: mattergen|diffcsp|adit|airss, checkpoint/model_name, guidance_scale/step_lr/cfg_scale, seed, conditioning: chemical_system|property|spacegroup+wyckoff} - is REPRESENTATION / provenance metadata attached to that Structure instance, exactly as a database provenance (mp-id) or a DFT-relaxation provenance would be. The map keeps Structure a source node; the generator is a source ANNOTATION, parallel to how mp-api structures enter as sourced instances.
generative_operator_proposal.if_the_map_later_wants_an_edge: IF Phase 2 makes generative producers first-class edges, the honest edge is generator -> Structure with the generator as an OPERATOR whose 'input' is a stochastic seed + optional conditioning LABEL and whose scheme carries (checkpoint, guidance, seed). It would still NOT be an edge from a property NODE; the conditioning stays a label. This inverts the current source topology and should be a deliberate kernel decision, not a side effect of ingesting these codes.
generative_operator_proposal.why_not_a_producing_edge_now: Adding generate_structure edges now would (a) invert the map's source topology for an artifact that is always immediately relaxed away, (b) grant node-like identity to an unverified proposal, and (c) mis-model steering labels as consumed fields. Provenance-on-instance captures the real usage (input artifact for a relax->screen chain) without any of these.

new_node_candidates.verdict: NONE recommended as physics nodes. All generative-side quantities are dimensionless scores or categorical labels -> representation-only.
new_node_candidates.space_group_symmetry: Space-group number / Wyckoff spec (diffcsp input, pyxtal tables). A CATEGORICAL annotation (integer in [1,230] + Wyckoff letters), not a physical quantity with a dimension. Representation-only: a symmetry label on a Structure instance, NOT a node. (It is an INPUT constraint to diffcsp, an emergent property after relaxation elsewhere.)
new_node_candidates.validity_score_smact: smact composition validity: a DIMENSION-FREE boolean over compositions. Not a physics quantity; a filter verdict. Representation-only IF ever surfaced; not a node.
new_node_candidates.novelty_metric: mat-structure-novelty output (matched / novel vs MP+ICSD via StructureMatcher, tolerances ltol=0.2 stol=0.3 angle_tol=5.0). A DIMENSIONLESS categorical verdict computed by a downstream FILTER, not a producer. Representation-only: a novelty flag on a Structure instance. NOT a node.
new_node_candidates.stability_gates: E_hull / formation_energy already exist as nodes (energy_above_hull, formation_energy in registry.py:95-96); the generative pipeline CONSUMES those as screening gates - no new node needed.

traps: TRAP 0 (brief correction): pyxtal is NOT used to generate structures in AtomisticSkills. Its from_random capability is unused; the AIRSS-style 'random symmetric structures' come from pymatgen (Structure.from_spacegroup / SpaceGroup.from_int_number), not pyxtal. pyxtal appears only as a Wyckoff-operator table inside diffcsp's wrapper.

TRAP 1 (brief correction): smact is env-only; NO skill script calls it. The charge-balance screening that DOES run (mat-ionic-substitution) is pymatgen's AutoOxiStateDecorationTransformation, not smact.

TRAP 2 - fractional vs cartesian: generate_random_structures.py builds coords with coords_are_cartesian=False (random fractional). All generators emit CIF (fractional). A comparator must not assume cartesian; cell + fractional coords define the structure.

TRAP 3 - cell conventions / P1 vs symmetric: the AIRSS random structures are P1 (no symmetry enforced; symmetry only emerges after relaxation, SKILL.md:82). diffcsp INJECTS symmetry via Wyckoff ops. So two generators disagree on whether the pre-relaxation cell carries symmetry - do not assume a generated cell is in a symmetrized/standardized setting.

TRAP 4 - charge-balance rules (smact): smact validity requires BOTH charge neutrality AND electronegativity ordering per assigned oxidation states; a composition can be charge-balanced yet smact-invalid (electronegativity order) or vice versa. Dimension-free, oxidation-state dependent. Distinct from pymatgen charge balance (mat-ionic-substitution), which only enforces neutrality on decorated oxidation states.

TRAP 5 - chemical_system != stoichiometry (mattergen): chemical_system='Li-Zr-Cl' controls ELEMENTS not exact composition and may omit an element or add none; mandatory post-filter of CIFs by exact elements (SKILL.md:131-145). A map must not read chemical_system conditioning as a guaranteed composition.

TRAP 6 - CSP vs Gen models: diffcsp CSP models (mp_csp) REQUIRE atom_types (exact composition); Gen models (mp_gen) generate without them. mattergen public models are Gen/conditioned only - true CSP (exact composition) needs non-public checkpoints (SKILL.md:141-146). 'Generated with target composition' is NOT achievable with public mattergen.

TRAP 7 - guidance/step scheme parameters are NOT physics: guidance_scale (mattergen), step_lr (diffcsp Langevin), cfg_scale (adit) are sampler/steering knobs. They belong in the generation SCHEME/provenance, never as physical fields.

TRAP 8 - none of the four codes import in the local miniconda base; mattergen/diffcsp are git-only (repos at /home/bdeng/...). Facts are anchored to committed AtomisticSkills usage, which is where the map-relevant behavior lives.
open_questions: 1. Provenance vs edge: is a source ANNOTATION on Structure instances the right Phase-1 model (recommended), or does the map want a first-class generator->Structure edge that inverts the source topology? This is a kernel decision, not an ingest side-effect.

2. If provenance: what is the schema of a generation-provenance record? Proposed: {generator_code, checkpoint/model_name, conditioning (chemical_system|property|spacegroup+wyckoff|none), sampler_scheme (guidance_scale|step_lr|cfg_scale), seed}. Parallel to a database (mp-id) provenance and a relaxation provenance.

3. Space group as annotation vs node: confirm SG/Wyckoff stays a categorical representation label (recommended) rather than becoming a quantity node. It is an INPUT constraint to diffcsp but an emergent property after relaxation - the map may want a single symmetry-annotation slot on Structure that both can write.

4. Novelty flag placement: mat-structure-novelty produces a matched/novel verdict against MP+ICSD. Is that a representation flag on a Structure instance (recommended) or does the map want a Novelty relation edge (this_Structure MATCHES that_Structure) - a Structure-to-Structure comparator, akin to an EXPECTED_AGREE relation?

5. smact: ingest at all? It is dimension-free and not skill-driven. Recommend NOT ingesting as a node; if a composition-validity annotation is ever wanted it is a label on a Composition, and even Composition is not yet a first-class map node.
6. Molecule branch (adit molecules): out of Phase-1 crystalline scope; note as a future non-periodic Structure sibling only.

7. Cross-generator diversity: the workflows deliberately run mattergen + diffcsp + adit for DIVERSITY on the same target. Is there an EXPECTED-DISAGREE / diversity relation worth modeling (unlike MLIP EXPECTED_AGREE, generators are meant to DISAGREE)? Likely representation-only, but noted.
summary.nothing_blocked: No source was inaccessible for the map-relevant facts. The four packages do not import locally and mattergen/diffcsp are git-only, but all functional usage lives in vendored AtomisticSkills skills, wrappers, servers, and env docs, every claim anchored to a real file:line. pyxtal/smact were not pip-downloaded because they are env-only in AtomisticSkills (pyxtal: one Wyckoff-table call; smact: zero call sites).
